orientation: Read this file first. Every rule here is non-negotiable. Violating any rule wastes Commander's time.

commander.name: Daniel
commander.cognitiveProfile: Pattern-recognition thinker. Two modes: extracting (discussion, questions, testing — NOT a request to act) and applying (moves fast, no hand-holding). Recognize which mode he is in.
rules: Discussion is not permission to execute. Wait for explicit signal: go, do it, proceed.
Never move on without being told.
Never add, remove, or change anything he didn't ask for.
After every change, immediately show the updated table.
If unsure what he means, ask. Don't guess.
No hedging, no softening, no restating what he said.
No unsolicited suggestions, no extra cleanup, no improvements he didn't ask for.
commander.stakes: Blockify must succeed so he can stay home with his sick wife. Every wasted minute costs him. Get it right.

blockify.description: Drag-and-drop builder that exports owned Shopify Liquid code. Elements on a canvas export as Liquid HTML, CSS with Liquid variables, and Shopify schema JSON.
blockify.differentiator: Merchant pastes exported code into Shopify. It works immediately. They can uninstall the app and keep everything. No lock-in.

threeLocations.description: Every template property exists in up to 3 places. These are the ONLY places that matter for templates.

json.role: Single source of truth. styleOverrides for styles, propOverrides for content. Also has responsiveStyles for xs/sm viewports.

filterMap.role: Gates Path 1. Properties listed here become Liquid vars with Shopify schema controls.

templateCSS.role: Handles Path 2. Properties here become hardcoded static CSS in the export.

fourRoutes.description: Every property follows exactly one route.
fourRoutes.route1_userOptIn: Property exists in panel, no default, no toggle. Inert until user acts.
fourRoutes.route2_defaultEditable: In panel, toggled on by default, has a value, always in schema. Requires getDefaultStyle or getDefaultProps, getDefaultSchemaToggles set to true, and canBeSchemaEditable: true in elementDefinitions.
fourRoutes.route3_structural: Not in panel. Not editable. Hardcoded CSS only. Things like display:flex, box-sizing, cursor:pointer.
fourRoutes.templateRoute: Adds filtering on top of Routes 1-3. Every property is in exactly one of two places: filter map (editable, Liquid var) or templateCSS (hardcoded). Nothing in neither. Nothing in both. Ever.

path1.name: Filter Map → Liquid var
path1.flow: Property in filter map → getElementDef keeps it → cssGeneration.js writes Liquid var → schemaGeneration.js writes schema JSON → EXPORT
path1.requirement: Value MUST exist in JSON styleOverrides or propOverrides. Filter map without value = empty Liquid var.

path2.name: templateCSS → Hardcoded CSS
path2.flow: Property NOT in filter map → templateCSS.js writes it as static CSS → EXPORT
path2.requirement: Value MUST exist in JSON. templateCSS without JSON = no source of truth.

invariants: Every exported value must have schema editable checked.
Zero values (0, 0px, empty string) are valid — never strip them.
No property can be both in the filter map and hardcoded in templateCSS.
xl and sm go through schema as Liquid vars — do NOT hardcode them in templateCSS.
Every JSON property must also be in filter map OR templateCSS. Never JSON only.
Every templateCSS property must also be in JSON. Never templateCSS only.
Every filter map property must also be in JSON. Never filter map only.

commands.remove: Delete from every place the property appears — JSON, filter map, templateCSS. If it's in 2 places, remove from 2. If it's in 3, remove from 3.
commands.moveToFilterMap: Add to filter map in elementDefinitions.js. Remove from templateCSS.js. Leave JSON alone.
commands.moveToTemplateCSS: Add to templateCSS.js. Remove from filter map in elementDefinitions.js. Leave JSON alone.
commands.showTable: Read the current state of all 3 files. Show audit table with columns: Property, JSON, Filter Map, templateCSS, Path. Value or dash. No parentheticals, no annotations, no invented labels.
commands.showUpdatedTable: After EVERY change, immediately show the updated table. No exceptions. Don't make Commander ask.
commands.showNextTable: Move to the next element. Only when Commander says.

pathValues.1: In filter map (Liquid var, editable in Shopify)
pathValues.BOTH: In filter map AND templateCSS — this is a violation, must be resolved

rules: Show value or dash. Nothing else.
No parentheticals, no emphasis, no invented labels.
xs and md viewports are not relevant to the audit. Do not include them.
sm IS relevant — it goes through schema as Liquid var.

responsiveViewports.xs: Hardcoded CSS, only if user set value. Not in audit tables.
responsiveViewports.md: Hardcoded CSS, only if user set value. Not in audit tables.
responsiveViewports.lg: Base CSS (no media query), desktop schema breakpoint.

templateWork.neverTouchBaseElements: When working on templates, NEVER modify base element definitions (getDefaultStyle, getDefaultProps, getDefaultSchemaToggles, or base styleProps/contentProps in elementDefinitions.js). Only touch: template JSON, template filter map entry, template templateCSS function.
templateWork.columnSchemaToggles: Auto-generated from filter map in templateUtils.js. No manual schemaToggles in JSON. Column filter map applies to ALL columns — every column needs values in columnStyles.
templateWork.beforeAnyWork: Read the template JSON, filter map, and templateCSS. Verify the 3 are in sync. Do not rely on memory.

standardEditableSets.note: Exceptions to standard sets are decided by Commander only.

keyFiles.cssGeneration: app/_utils/cssGeneration.js — CSS export, Liquid vars, calls templateCSS
keyFiles.templateUtils: app/_utils/templateUtils.js — Template instantiation, auto-generates schema toggles from filter map
